title: Listing Addressees — 'Distribution:' Line Only
plain_language_rule: When a multiple-address letter has more than four action addressees, or when the number of copies to any addressee varies, omit the "To:" line and use only a "Distribution:" line. Addressees shown in the "Distribution:" line are action addressees. Addressees may be listed by SNDL short title, collective title, or both. Distribution and Copy to addressees are usually listed in single columns, but may be listed in paragraphs or columns to avoid page breaks.
source_text: 8-2b. Using a "Distribution:" Line Only. Omit the "To:" line and add a "Distribution:" line if you have more than four action addressees or if you vary the number of copies to any of the addressees. Addressees shown in a "Distribution:" line are action addressees. You may list addressees in the "Distribution:" line by: (a) SNDL short titles; (b) collective titles; or (c) both collective and SNDL short title. Usually list "Distribution" and "Copy to:" addressees in single columns. Addressees may be listed in paragraphs or columns to keep a letter from going to another page.
renderer_impact: This rule selects the address-line format rather than prescribing specific layout or rendering. The renderer must check the addressee count and copy-variance condition to determine whether the 'Distribution:' Line Only format applies. Specific line placement, spacing, and column layout are determined by figure 8-2 and are not encoded as machine-validatable constraints in this rule.
notes: Source text resolved 2026-05-11. The 'more than four' condition in 8-2b is stated as a condition (whereas 8-2a uses 'good rule-of-thumb'); ambiguity preserved nonetheless per manual language. The addressee-count threshold triggers the format switch; the alternative being the 'To:' Line Only format (C8-002) or both lines (C8-004). Relationship to Chapter 7: addressees in the Distribution line are action addressees, analogous to the 'To:' line addressees in C8-002; the 'Copy to:' addressees remain information-only recipients. This is a format-selection rule, not a layout-validation rule.
